evidence: ExplorationHandlers → ExploreResponseBuilder seam is clean: handlers deal only with HttpExchange + params; ExploreResponseBuilder handles all model-to-JSON translation. No scattered mapping code.
ValidatedExploreRequest record bundles pre-validated state (seedsRaw, relationConfig, exploreParams) so handler methods don't re-extract parameters inline.
GrammarConfigLoader → GrammarConfig seam is clean: loader owns I/O and parsing; config owns querying. Handlers accept GrammarConfig — no loader reference escapes the startup wiring.

handleSemanticFieldComparison does not use validateExploreRequest (it doesn't need a RelationConfig) but reimplements ExploreParams resolution by calling parseIntParam('top') and parseDoubleParam('min_logdice') directly — minor seam inconsistency.

parseIntParam and parseDoubleParam are private to ExplorationHandlers but ConcordanceHandlers needs the same pattern, causing it to re-implement inline parsing with its own try/catch. The parameter-parsing seam is not properly bounded.
ExploreOptions record is the parameter carrier across the api→exploration boundary — clean, minimal, named fields.
SemanticFieldExplorer is a clean injection point: ExplorationHandlers accepts SemanticFieldExplorer (not BlackLabQueryExecutor directly), so test doubles can be injected without standing up BlackLab.
Model-layer types (ExplorationResult, ComparisonResult) are plain data objects with no HTTP concerns — the boundary is clean in both directions.

strengths: ExploreResponseBuilder centralises all model-to-JSON translation for exploration endpoints — zero scattered mapping code.
ValidatedExploreRequest record makes the 'parse + validate' phase explicit and reusable across three handlers.
GrammarConfigLoader / GrammarConfig separation keeps I/O concerns out of the handlers completely.
SemanticFieldExplorer injection seam supports testing without a live corpus index.
ExploreOptions carries the api→exploration parameter boundary cleanly with named components.
mid_level_elegance.issue_character: The mid-level issues are localized to the parameter-parsing seam: numeric param helpers are private to one handler class but needed by siblings, and one handler partially re-implements validation inline rather than using the shared preamble.
mid_level_elegance.score_rationale: The major handoffs (api↔exploration, api↔config, exploration↔query) are well-choreographed with clear translation points. The score is held below 85 by the parameter-parsing seam leak: parseIntParam/parseDoubleParam are private to ExplorationHandlers but semantically belong at the HttpApiUtils boundary, causing ConcordanceHandlers to re-implement inline and handleSemanticFieldComparison to partially bypass the validated-request preamble.

issues.summary: Numeric param parsing helpers are scoped to ExplorationHandlers but leaked as inline duplicates into sibling handler classes
evidence: ExplorationHandlers.parseIntParam and parseDoubleParam are private methods.

ConcordanceHandlers.handleConcordanceExamples contains: 'try { top = Integer.parseInt(params.getOrDefault("top", "10")); } catch (NumberFormatException e) { sendError(400, ...) }' — identical logic, different error message.
The natural home for these helpers is HttpApiUtils, which is already the shared HTTP utility boundary used by all handlers.
issues.suggestion: Add static parseIntParam(HttpExchange, Map<String,String>, String, int) and parseDoubleParam variants to HttpApiUtils (package-visible). Remove private versions from ExplorationHandlers. ConcordanceHandlers replaces inline try/catch with the shared helper.

issues.summary: handleSemanticFieldComparison partially re-implements parameter resolution rather than using the shared validated-request preamble
evidence: handleSemanticFieldExplore and handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti both call validateExploreRequest() which bundles seeds, relation, and params.

handleSemanticFieldComparison calls parseBaseExploreRequest() + requireParam('seeds') + resolveExploreParams() separately — it cannot use validateExploreRequest because it doesn't need a RelationConfig.
The resulting inline code is structurally similar but not identical to the validated-request path, creating slight divergence in error responses.
issues.suggestion: Introduce a lighter-weight ValidatedComparisonRequest record (seeds, exploreParams) and a corresponding validateComparisonRequest() method that handles the comparison-specific preamble. This gives handleSemanticFieldComparison its own clean preamble without forcing it to reuse the full exploration flow.

root_causes: parseIntParam/parseDoubleParam were added to ExplorationHandlers when they should have been added to HttpApiUtils from the start
likely_symptoms: param-parse-seam-leak is the same root cause as double-sentinel-contract in batch 5
